message:
perf(read-tool): zero-allocation line counting via memchr + lazy line iteration

- src/tools.rs: replace Vec<&str> collect-all-lines approach with
  memchr::memchr_iter byte scanning for O(1) memory line counting,
  then lazy split('\n').skip(start).take(count) for the selected
  window only. Eliminates the up-front allocation of a Vec holding
  every line reference in the file — significant for large files
  where only a small window is requested (e.g., offset=5000 limit=50
  on a 100K-line file previously allocated 100K pointers).
- Preserves existing behavior: trailing-newline phantom line removal,
  offset/limit clamping, line number formatting, truncation detection
- tests/artifacts/perf: updated golden outputs reflecting latest
  benchmark run (minor variance in timing microseconds)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
